content.full_text:
FPPC

JUL 20 8:28 AM '90

Law Offices of
Markman, Arczynski, Hansen & Goldman
A Professional Corporation

JAMES L. MARKMAN
ANDREW ARCZYNSKI
RALPH D. HANSEN
F. ELLIOT GOLDMAN
JEFFREY KING
D. CRAIG FOX
MARTHA GEISLER PATTERSON
WILLIAM P. CURLEY III

NUMBER ONE CIVIC CENTER CIRCLE
P.O. BOX 1059
BREA, CALIFORNIA 92622-1059
TELEPHONE (714) 990-0801
        (213) 691-3811

9113 FOOTHILL BOULEVARD
SUITE 200
RANCHO CUCAMONGA, CALIFORNIA 91730
TELEPHONE (714) 980-2742
        (714) 381-0216

IN REPLY PLEASE REFER TO:

Brea

July 19, 1990


Scott Hallbrin
Acting General Counsel
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Request for assistance pertaining to potential
    conflict of interest

Dear Mr. Hallbrin:

     The undersigned serves in the capacity of City
Attorney to the City of Diamond Bar. It is in that capacity
that I am corresponding with you regarding a potential conflict
of interest regarding a specific member of that City Council,
Council Member Phyllis Papen. Ms. Papen has requested this
correspondence be directed to you as the result of telephone
conversations between our respective offices, as well as
telephone discussions between Ms. Papen, the undersigned and
Deanna Stone, Staff Counsel to the Commission. We are
enclosing herewith, a copy of a letter directed to Council
Member Papen, in her then-capacity as Mayor of Diamond
Bar dated April 17, 1990. However, in order to assist
in your analysis of the issue, the following facts are
presented:

     Council Member Papen was elected and assumed office
as a member of the City Council of the City of Diamond Bar in
April, 1989. During the course of the preceding six months,
the City has embarked upon a process of selecting a franchise
refuse hauler or haulers with respect to solid waste handling
within the community. At least in part, that process is in
response to the requirements of Assembly Bill 939 which
requires the City to meet specified State mandates with respect
to reductions in solid waste materials deposited in landfills.
The City Staff, at the direction of the City Council, issued
Requests for Proposal to various solid waste enterprises both

Scott Hallbrin
July 19, 1990
Page Two


currently working within the community and those not presently
working in the community. It is the intent of the City Council
to establish, by contract, a franchise hauler or haulers by the
end of August, 1990.

Council Member Papen presently resides with a
gentleman who is not her spouse. The gentleman is a marketing
consultant. In that capacity, he has provided marketing
services to two of the competing franchise haulers interested
in the solid waste franchise with the City of Diamond Bar.
Accordingly the question presented is whether or not Council
Member Papen should abstain from the selection process due to a
conflict of interest pursuant to the Political Reform Act,
California Government Code Sections 81000, et seq.

As indicated above, and in our correspondence to Ms.
Papen of April 17, 1990, a copy of which is attached, Ms. Papen
and the gentlemen with whom she resides do not co-mingle assets,
maintain joint checking or savings accounts, or other financial
accounts, but do, occasionally, exchange "gifts" of a personal
nature, including , but not limited to, entertainment, meals,
personal property and/or expenses involved in recreational
travel. In addition, the gentlemen with who Ms. Papen resides
provides his "pro rata" share of the expenses of maintaining
the residence, including his fair share of the rental
obligation therefore.

There would appear to be little, if any, doubt that
were Ms. Papen married to the gentleman in question, then the
income which the individual receives from the solid waste
enterprise would be, at least partially, attributable to her as
a source of income pursuant to the provisions of Section 82030
of the Political Reform Act. However, based upon our
conversations with your office, and our review of the pertinent
authorities and regulations, it would appear that the income
analysis only goes to the issue of whether or not the
individual is the spouse of the public official, or other close
family member as defined in the regulations and the law.

As noted above, the City Council is moving forward
with the issue of a contract for solid waste services within
the community in the very near future. The City Council will,
hopefully, be meeting on July 31, for purposes of reviewing a
final list of the competing proposals to determine the
specific needs of the City. Again, it is hoped that the
contract for such services will be finally negotiated and
entered into prior to the end of August, 1990. Accordingly, we
would respectfully request that the advice provided by your

Scott Hallbrin
July 19, 1990
Page Two

office be forwarded to the undersigned at your earliest
possible convenience.

Should you have any other questions or desire further
information regarding the topic for purposes of your analysis,
please do not hesitate to contact the undersigned at your
earliest convenience.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Andrew V. Arczynski
City Attorney
City of Diamond Bar

AVA:clf
Encl.
C:\1011\HALLAB

Law Offices of
Markman, Arczynski, Hanson & Goldman
A Professional Corporation

JAMES L. MARKMAN
ANDREW W. ARCZYNSKI
RALPH D. HANSON
F. ELLIOT GOLDMAN
JEFFREY KING
D. CRAIG FOX
MARTHA GEISLER PATTERSON
WILLIAM P. CURLEY III

NUMBER ONE CIVIC CENTER CIRCLE
P. O. BOX 1059
BREA, CALIFORNIA 92622-1059

TELEPHONE: (714) 990-0901
         (213) 691-3811

9113 FOOTHILL BOULEVARD
SUITE 200
RANCHO CUCAMONGA, CALIFORNIA 91730

TELEPHONE: (714) 980-2742
         (714) 381-0218

IN REPLY PLEASE REFER TO:

Brea

August 13, 1990

Blanca Breeze
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
Legal Division
428 "J" Street
Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Letter No. 90-475; Supplemental Information

Dear Ms. Breeze:

     Pursuant to our telephone conversation on August 10, 1990, I submit to you the following supplemental information. Today I spoke with Council Member Papen in regards to certain issues which we discussed in our telephone conversation. The supplemental information I have is as follows:

     (a) Ownership of Residence: Council Member Papen informed me that the residence in which they residence is rented on a month-to-month basis. Initially Ms. Papen had entered into a one year lease with the owner wherein she was the signatory to the lease agreement. The lease commenced January 15, 1988 and ran for a one year period. No formal substitute lease agreement has been entered into; Ms. Papen retains her occupancy on a month-to-month basis.

     (b) Gifts: Ms. Papen has stated that she has not received any gifts which in the aggregate exceed $250.00 from her "significant other".

     I hope this information will assist you in your analysis of the issues presented by this matter. I will look forward to your response; you indicated that the earliest we may see it is Friday, August 17, 1990. If you have any

Blanca Breeze
August 13, 1990
Page Two


additional questions or if I may be of any service to you
please contact me at your convenience.  Thank you in advance
for your assistance in this matter.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
William P. Curley, III
Deputy City Attorney
City of Diamond Bar

C/1011/LBREEZE

sections.question_synthetic: Does Council Member Papen have a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when the gentleman she resides with (non-spouse) provides marketing services to competing solid waste franchise haulers seeking a contract with the city?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request from City Attorney Andrew V. Arczynski to the FPPC, not an FPPC response/advice letter. The document includes a follow-up supplemental information letter dated August 13, 1990. No FPPC conclusion section is present. OCR quality is moderate with some character recognition issues.

embedding.summary: An incoming request from the City Attorney of Diamond Bar seeking FPPC advice regarding whether Council Member Phyllis Papen should abstain from voting on a solid waste franchise selection due to a potential conflict of interest. The request details that Papen resides with a marketing consultant who has provided services to two competing franchise haulers.
